finding: edge-tts: Free, no API key, GPL-3.0, 11.3k stars, es-CO voices, streaming capable
ElevenLabs: WebSocket streaming, ~200ms TTFB, Free tier 10k credits/mo, paid from $6/mo, excellent quality
Cartesia: sonic-3/3.5 models, ultra-low latency, emotion control, streaming API, supports es
Deepgram: aura-2 models, low latency, but max 2000 chars, primarily English focused
Piper: Fast local neural TTS, GPL-3.0, 4.5k stars (piper1-gpl), very low latency, community voices
Coqui TTS: XTTS v2 streams <200ms, 16 languages incl Spanish, MPL-2.0, 45.6k stars, company shut down but OSS
Kokoro: 82M params, Apache-2.0, 7.5k stars, very fast, Spanish support (lang_code=e), lightweight
Fish Speech S2: 4B params, SOTA quality, ~100ms TTFA on H200, 80+ languages, but needs heavy GPU
StyleTTS2: Human-level quality, MIT license, 6.3k stars, style diffusion, but English-focused, needs training
Best for our use case: edge-tts (free, Colombian voices, streaming) + Kokoro (local fallback, Apache-2.0)
